name: ai-maestro-code-graph-query
description: PROACTIVELY query the code graph database to understand relationships and impact of changes. Use this skill WHEN READING any file to understand context, when searching for files, when exploring the codebase, or when you need to understand what depends on a component. This is your primary tool for understanding code structure and avoiding breaking changes.
capabilities: Find all functions that call a given function (callers)
Find all functions called by a given function (callees)
Delta-index changed files for quick updates
author: 23blocks-OS
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. All 79 static findings are false positives caused by markdown documentation patterns. The skill instructs AI agents to run local shell scripts from ~/.local/bin/ and make localhost API calls to port 23000. No external network access or sensitive data exfiltration.
valueStatement: Understanding code relationships prevents breaking changes. This skill automatically queries a code graph database to find callers, callees, serializers, and associations before you modify any component.
limitations: Requires AI Maestro dashboard running on localhost
Requires graph database to be indexed first
Only works with code files in indexed projects
Cannot query non-code files or binary data

useCases.title: Safe Model Changes
useCases.description: Before modifying a model, find all serializers and associations that might break.

useCases.title: Impact Analysis
useCases.description: Understand the full impact of changing a core function before refactoring.

useCases.title: Architecture Discovery
useCases.description: Explore unknown codebases by tracing relationships between components.

promptTemplates.title: Understand a File
promptTemplates.prompt: Read this file, then query the graph to find: what depends on this component, what it depends on, and any serializers or associations.

promptTemplates.title: Before Refactoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all callers of this function before making changes. List each caller and its file location.

promptTemplates.title: Find Serializers
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all serializers for this model. Check if any serializers might be missing from the codebase.

promptTemplates.title: Trace Call Path
promptTemplates.prompt: Find the call path from the API controller entry point to this internal function.

bestPractices: Query the graph immediately after reading any code file to build context
Check serializers before modifying database models
Verify all callers before changing function signatures
antiPatterns: Skipping graph queries for simple changes
Making modifications without checking for downstream effects
Assuming you understand the full impact without querying

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It requires the AI Maestro dashboard running locally.

faq.question: How does the code graph get built?
faq.answer: The graph is built by indexing your codebase. Run graph-index-delta.sh to update it with changes.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with any language. Language detection depends on your AI Maestro installation configuration.

faq.question: Is my code data sent externally?
faq.answer: No. All graph data stays on your machine. The API calls are to localhost port 23000 only.

faq.question: Why are my queries returning no results?
faq.answer: The graph needs to be indexed first. Run graph-index-delta.sh on your project directory.

faq.question: How is this different from grep or search?
faq.answer: Graph queries understand relationships. Find all callers, not just text matches. Shows call paths and dependencies.
